FNF invest in structures that facilitate either self-liquidation or structured equity exits.
FSDAi should be able to receive the invested amount plus any declared dividend payments by the end of the 7th year of investment (2028).
Investment in LMFCF will be redeemed at the end of the fund life in June 2027. Dividends are distributed quarterly.
Potential	exit	after 3	years	(repayment	of	Note)	or 7 years (following	conversion)

Preference shares are redeemable by the company at the option of the investor any time after the fifth anniversary at a price that will give the investor a minimum return of 6% in USD terms, net of all taxes and charges.

The debt instrument will be repaid in a bullet payment after 10 years. 
The Fund may not prepay all or any part of the sub debt until the 5th anniversary of the first disbursement, May 2023. The Fund may prepay, after the 5th anniversary and on the interest, payment dates (15th March and 15th September) all or any part of the loan, including accrued interest, and a prepayment premium equal to 1% of the amount to be prepaid. Any amount prepaid may not be reborrowed by the Fund.
The exit of FSDAi’s indirect exposure in iBuild Home Loans was completed in January 2023

The exit will be in 2027 with the option to extend subject to majority vote by the LPAC. Further, FSDAi’s investment will be recovered at the end of the Fund and the NAV will be determined in accordance with the Luxembourg Law, the Partnership Agreement, and the Lux GAAP.

Blue Orchard Emerging Markets MSME liquidity facility was conceived out of the liquidity and solvency challenges faced by strong microfinance institutions (“MFIs”) because of the Covid-19 crisis.

Lendable is the first debt platform designed to finance specifically the new generation of tech enabled, African non-bank lenders. Lendable will provide term loans to Alternative financial 
institutions.
Provide SMEs with working capital financing through a true sale of receivables.
Carbon Value Exchange Ltd, is a private company limited by shares registered in England and Wales.
Catalyst Fund Resilience I, L.P. (“Catalyst Fund” or the “Fund”) is registered as a Delaware limited partnership.

Catalyst Fund is a pre-seed high-impact fund backing tech startups that seek to improve the resilience of underserved and vulnerable communities in Africa, with a focus on fintech and climate adaptation solutions
FSDAi’s investment is ring-fenced to SSA
Projects supplying carbon credits will be based in the global south (Africa, Latin America and Southeast Asia) whilst buyers will be global. Initial use case development is mainly focused on Africa.
ACV will operate as a venture builder to develop and invest in climate businesses with potential for significant scale which play a role in carbon mitigation and removal.

FCC issues guarantees against local currency collateral assets (e.g., treasury bonds) held by the financial institution (Obligors), to allow the financial institution to access liquidity in the money market by borrowing from other banks (Beneficiaries).

ImFact intends to provide SMEs with off balance sheet working capital financing through a true sale of receivables. ImFact will purchase receivables from SMEs at 40% to 60% of the asset face value thereby shortening their cash conversion cycle and availing capital for growth.
In support of Alternative Local Capital Partners (ALCPs)
Lendable is an innovative fund manager that has specialised in providing financing to fintech companies in sub-Saharan Africa ("SSA").

Projects engaged in carbon mitigation and removal activities will supply their carbon credits directly through the CaVEx platform. Edge intelligence will be used to remotely capture real time data on project micro activity. The activity based data will thereafter be used to create accurate digital carbon credits.
Initial project use cases include clean cook stoves, solar water pumps, forestry, 
electric bikes, waste to energy biogas and solar battery chargers.
Risk capital to enable emerging local capital providers serving small and growing businesses in Africa to test new funding models and attract capital thereafter.
